svg.text: Wiring the KY-022 (VS1838B) IR receiver to the Radxa Dragon Q6A 40-pin GPIO header
Radxa Dragon Q6A — 40-pin GPIO header (board rotated 90° CW)
to IR module S (yellow wire) · active-low
All three pins sit in the LEFT column (odd row) on the header.
Viewed top-down with U.FL connectors up: pin 1 at top, pin 9 two rows below, pin 15 five rows below pin 9.
Pin numbering per radxa_dragon_q6a_schematic_v1.21.pdf and docs.radxa.com
GPIO_1 kernel line: tlmm gpiochip4 offset 1 · header silk: "PIN_15" · primary alt-fn: plain GPIO
⚠ Do NOT use Pin 3 (boot hang) or Pin 5 (I2C7_SCL, unreliable for IR).
Waveshare RS485 CAN HAT-B uses pins 11/12/19/21–24/35–38/40 — our trio (1, 9, 15) is clear.
VS1838B is open-collector with an internal pull-up.
Idle = HIGH (≈VCC), pulse = LOW. Wire the signal directly to GPIO_1 —
no level shifter or external pull-up needed (the Q6A header is already
level-shifted from the QCS6490's 1.8 V core to 3.3 V).
KY-022 (TL1838 / VS1838B) — back view, looking at the header pins
Verified head-on against the physical module — silk reads (left → right): − , + , S.
Linux side — gpio-ir-recv overlay + IR remote keymap
Companion: 60-playbill-ir-keymap.rules runs ir-keytable -c -p nec -w at boot.
protocol = "nec" · 11 scancodes: ▲▼◀▶ OK Back Home Menu Vol+/− Power
Power → KEY_PROG1 (XF86Launch1) → GNOME launches Playbill (does NOT poweroff)
Scancodes are remote-specific — recapture with `ir-keytable -t -p nec` if remote changes.
